The **2015 NRL season** was the 108th season of professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") in Australia and the 18th season of the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") in Australia and New Zealand. The season started in [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand") with the annual [Auckland Nines](/wiki/2015_NRL_Auckland_Nines "2015 NRL Auckland Nines"), which was followed by the [Rugby League All Stars Match](/wiki/2015_All_Stars_match "2015 All Stars match"), returning after a one-year hiatus, and the [World Club Series](/wiki/2015_World_Club_Series "2015 World Club Series").[[1]](#cite_note-NRL2015-1) [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") won their first premiership in only the second Grand Final to be decided in extra time and their second ever grand final.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Season launch and advertising

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_NRL_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Season launch and advertising")]

The season launched with a gala event at the [Queens Wharf, Auckland](/wiki/Queens_Wharf,_Auckland "Queens Wharf, Auckland") on 29 January attended by a selection of the code's stars, including [Shaun Johnson](/wiki/Shaun_Johnson "Shaun Johnson") and [Greg Inglis](/wiki/Greg_Inglis "Greg Inglis"). It was the first time the event had been held outside Australia.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The NRL and their longstanding ad agency MJW Haukuhodo used, for the second year running, [Robbie Williams](/wiki/Robbie_Williams "Robbie Williams")' "[Let Me Entertain You](/wiki/Let_Me_Entertain_You_(Robbie_Williams_song) "Let Me Entertain You (Robbie Williams song)")" as the commercial soundtrack. The launch commercial takes members and supporters on a journey from the outback plains of Western Queensland to an inner city boot camp in Sydney and features more than fifty NRL club members and fans in a range of scenarios that include a rendition of a [Haka](/wiki/Haka "Haka") on a Sydney train and a focus on [Greg Inglis](/wiki/Greg_Inglis "Greg Inglis")' trademark Goanna post-[try celebration](/wiki/Try_celebration "Try celebration").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The second Sunday afternoon match began at 4:10 pm instead of the traditional 3:10 pm. This change was to allow the [Nine Network](/wiki/Nine_Network "Nine Network") to show the match live in New South Wales and Queensland, and use it as a lead-in to their respective [6:00 pm news bulletins](/wiki/Nine_News "Nine News").

## Pre-season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_NRL_season&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Pre-season")]

The 2015 pre-season featured the second [Auckland Nines](/wiki/Auckland_Nines "Auckland Nines") tournament, held over a weekend at Auckland's [Eden Park](/wiki/Eden_Park "Eden Park") in which the [South Sydney Rabbitohs](/wiki/South_Sydney_Rabbitohs "South Sydney Rabbitohs") defeated the [Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks") in the final. This tournament took place on the last weekend of January, and the [All Stars match](/wiki/All_Stars_match "All Stars match") was played on Friday 13 February at [Cbus Super Stadium](/wiki/Robina_Stadium "Robina Stadium") in Robina.[[1]](#cite_note-NRL2015-1) The [2015 World Club Series](/wiki/2015_World_Club_Series "2015 World Club Series") took place in England with [2014 premiers](/wiki/2014_NRL_season "2014 NRL season") the [South Sydney Rabbitohs](/wiki/South_Sydney_Rabbitohs "South Sydney Rabbitohs") taking on [Super League champions](/wiki/Super_League_XIX "Super League XIX") [St. Helens](/wiki/St_Helens_R.F.C. "St Helens R.F.C.") in the showpiece match, which South Sydney won.

## Regular season
